{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|zh|方墳}} | name = Kamienyajizōyama Kofun | native_name =上塩冶地蔵山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamienyajizōyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kamienyajizōyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|20|45.95|N|132|45|34.15|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Izumo, Shimane]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|20|45.95|N|132|45|34.15|E|}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kamienyajizōyama Kofun'''|上塩冶地蔵山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamienya-chō, neighborhood of the city of [[Izumo, Shimane]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1924.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206143|title=上塩冶地蔵山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is ne of the Kofuns that make up the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Kamienyajizōyama Kofun is located on the north bank of the Kobe River, on a gentle slope on the west side of the Izumo Technical High School. in the northeastern part of Shimane Prefecture. Although the area around the tumulus has been largely leveled land clearing for [[pa11y field]]s, the tumulus is believed to be a square {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}-style ''kofun'', measuring 15 meters on each side, and with a height of five meters, although it may originally have been an oval-shaped tumulus. The horizontal stone [[burial chamber]] opens to the southeast, and consists of a burial chamber (back chamber), an antechamber, and a passageway. It was constructed with the same processed [[tuff]] stones as the [[Kamienyatsukiyama Kofun]] located 600 meters to the north. The burial chamber is about 9 meters long, 2.4 meters wide, and 2.25 meters high. The walls and ceiling of the burial chamber are each made of a single monolith of cut stone, and on the boundary with the antechamber in the foreground there is an entrance carved out of a single piece of stone. A house-shaped sarcophagus is placed in the back. As the tomb has been open since antiquity, no [[grave goods]] have been found, although no modern [[archaeological excavation]] has been conducted. From the structure of the tumulus, it is believed to have been built around the 7th century, or the final stages of the Kofun period. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is 1.7 kilometers, or approximately five minutes by car from [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]] [[Izumoshi Station]].
<gallery> Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, 3D-1.jpg|3D rendition of the burial chamber Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, zenshitsu-1.jpg|Antechamber（looking towards burial chamber） Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, zenshitsu-2.jpg| Antechamber（looking toward entrance） Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, sendou.jpg|Corridor（looking towards burial chamber） Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|zh|方墳}} | name = Kamienyajizōyama Kofun | native_name =上塩冶地蔵山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamienyajizōyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kamienyajizōyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|20|45.95|N|132|45|34.15|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Izumo, Shimane]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|20|45.95|N|132|45|34.15|E|}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kamienyajizōyama Kofun'''|上塩冶地蔵山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamienya-chō, neighborhood of the city of [[Izumo, Shimane]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1924.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206143|title=上塩冶地蔵山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is ne of the Kofuns that make up the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Kamienyajizōyama Kofun is located on the north bank of the Kobe River, on a gentle slope on the west side of the Izumo Technical High School. in the northeastern part of Shimane Prefecture. Although the area around the tumulus has been largely leveled land clearing for [[pa11y field]]s, the tumulus is believed to be a square {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun}}-style ''kofun'', measuring 15 meters on each side, and with a height of five meters, although it may originally have been an oval-shaped tumulus. The horizontal stone [[burial chamber]] opens to the southeast, and consists of a burial chamber (back chamber), an antechamber, and a passageway. It was constructed with the same processed [[tuff]] stones as the [[Kamienyatsukiyama Kofun]] located 600 meters to the north. The burial chamber is about 9 meters long, 2.4 meters wide, and 2.25 meters high. The walls and ceiling of the burial chamber are each made of a single monolith of cut stone, and on the boundary with the antechamber in the foreground there is an entrance carved out of a single piece of stone. A house-shaped sarcophagus is placed in the back. As the tomb has been open since antiquity, no [[grave goods]] have been found, although no modern [[archaeological excavation]] has been conducted. From the structure of the tumulus, it is believed to have been built around the 7th century, or the final stages of the Kofun period. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is 1.7 kilometers, or approximately five minutes by car from [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]] [[Izumoshi Station]].
<gallery> Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, 3D-1.jpg|3D rendition of the burial chamber Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, zenshitsu-1.jpg|Antechamber（looking towards burial chamber） Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, zenshitsu-2.jpg| Antechamber（looking toward entrance） Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, sendou.jpg|Corridor（looking towards burial chamber） Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

The {{nihongo|'''Kamienyajizōyama Kofun'''|上塩冶地蔵山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamienya-chō, neighborhood of the city of [[Izumo, Shimane]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1924.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206143|title=上塩冶地蔵山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is ne of the Kofuns that make up the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Kamienyajizōyama Kofun is located on the north bank of the Kobe River, on a gentle slope on the west side of the Izumo Technical High School. in the northeastern part of Shimane Prefecture. Although the area around the tumulus has been largely leveled land clearing for [[pa11y field]]s, the tumulus is believed to be a square {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style ''kofun'', measuring 15 meters on each side, and with a height of five meters, although it may originally have been an oval-shaped tumulus. The horizontal stone [[burial chamber]] opens to the southeast, and consists of a burial chamber (back chamber), an antechamber, and a passageway. It was constructed with the same processed [[tuff]] stones as the [[Kamienyatsukiyama Kofun]] located 600 meters to the north. The burial chamber is about 9 meters long, 2.4 meters wide, and 2.25 meters high. The walls and ceiling of the burial chamber are each made of a single monolith of cut stone, and on the boundary with the antechamber in the foreground there is an entrance carved out of a single piece of stone. A house-shaped sarcophagus is placed in the back. As the tomb has been open since antiquity, no [[grave goods]] have been found, although no modern [[archaeological excavation]] has been conducted. From the structure of the tumulus, it is believed to have been built around the 7th century, or the final stages of the Kofun period. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is 1.7 kilometers, or approximately five minutes by car from [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]] [[Izumoshi Station]].

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | name = Kamienyajizōyama Kofun | native_name =上塩冶地蔵山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamienyajizōyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kamienyajizōyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|20|45.95|N|132|45|34.15|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Izumo, Shimane]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|20|45.95|N|132|45|34.15|E|}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kamienyajizōyama Kofun'''|上塩冶地蔵山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamienya-chō, neighborhood of the city of [[Izumo, Shimane]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1924.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206143|title=上塩冶地蔵山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is ne of the Kofuns that make up the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Kamienyajizōyama Kofun is located on the north bank of the Kobe River, on a gentle slope on the west side of the Izumo Technical High School. in the northeastern part of Shimane Prefecture. Although the area around the tumulus has been largely leveled land clearing for [[pa11y field]]s, the tumulus is believed to be a square {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style ''kofun'', measuring 15 meters on each side, and with a height of five meters, although it may originally have been an oval-shaped tumulus. The horizontal stone [[burial chamber]] opens to the southeast, and consists of a burial chamber (back chamber), an antechamber, and a passageway. It was constructed with the same processed [[tuff]] stones as the [[Kamienyatsukiyama Kofun]] located 600 meters to the north. The burial chamber is about 9 meters long, 2.4 meters wide, and 2.25 meters high. The walls and ceiling of the burial chamber are each made of a single monolith of cut stone, and on the boundary with the antechamber in the foreground there is an entrance carved out of a single piece of stone. A house-shaped sarcophagus is placed in the back. As the tomb has been open since antiquity, no [[grave goods]] have been found, although no modern [[archaeological excavation]] has been conducted. From the structure of the tumulus, it is believed to have been built around the 7th century, or the final stages of the Kofun period. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is 1.7 kilometers, or approximately five minutes by car from [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]] [[Izumoshi Station]].
<gallery> Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, 3D-1.jpg|3D rendition of the burial chamber Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, zenshitsu-1.jpg|Antechamber（looking towards burial chamber） Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, zenshitsu-2.jpg| Antechamber（looking toward entrance） Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, sendou.jpg|Corridor（looking towards burial chamber） Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

The {{nihongo|'''Kamienyajizōyama Kofun'''|上塩冶地蔵山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamienya-chō, neighborhood of the city of [[Izumo, Shimane]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1924.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206143|title=上塩冶地蔵山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is ne of the Kofuns that make up the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Kamienyajizōyama Kofun is located on the north bank of the Kobe River, on a gentle slope on the west side of the Izumo Technical High School. in the northeastern part of Shimane Prefecture. Although the area around the tumulus has been largely leveled land clearing for [[pa11y field]]s, the tumulus is believed to be a square {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style ''kofun'', measuring 15 meters on each side, and with a height of five meters, although it may originally have been an oval-shaped tumulus. The horizontal stone [[burial chamber]] opens to the southeast, and consists of a burial chamber (back chamber), an antechamber, and a passageway. It was constructed with the same processed [[tuff]] stones as the [[Kamienyatsukiyama Kofun]] located 600 meters to the north. The walls and ceiling of the burial chamber are each made of a single monolith of cut stone, and on the boundary with the antechamber in the foreground there is an entrance carved out of a single piece of stone. A house-shaped sarcophagus is placed in the back. As the tomb has been open since antiquity, no [[grave goods]] have been found, although no modern [[archaeological excavation]] has been conducted. From the structure of the tumulus, it is believed to have been built around the 7th century, or the final stages of the Kofun period. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is 1.7 kilometers, or approximately five minutes by car from [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]] [[Izumoshi Station]].

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun}} | name = Kamienyajizōyama Kofun | native_name =上塩冶地蔵山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamienyajizōyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kamienyajizōyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|35|20|45.95|N|132|45|34.15|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Izumo, Shimane]], [[Japan]] | region = [[San'in region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|20|45.95|N|132|45|34.15|E|}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kamienyajizōyama Kofun'''|上塩冶地蔵山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamienya-chō, neighborhood of the city of [[Izumo, Shimane]] in the [[San'in region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1924.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206143|title=上塩冶地蔵山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is ne of the Kofuns that make up the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Kamienyajizōyama Kofun is located on the north bank of the Kobe River, on a gentle slope on the west side of the Izumo Technical High School. in the northeastern part of Shimane Prefecture. Although the area around the tumulus has been largely leveled land clearing for [[pa11y field]]s, the tumulus is believed to be a square {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style ''kofun'', measuring 15 meters on each side, and with a height of five meters, although it may originally have been an oval-shaped tumulus. The horizontal stone [[burial chamber]] opens to the southeast, and consists of a burial chamber (back chamber), an antechamber, and a passageway. It was constructed with the same processed [[tuff]] stones as the [[Kamienyatsukiyama Kofun]] located 600 meters to the north. The burial chamber is about 9 meters long, 2.4 meters wide, and 2.25 meters high. The walls and ceiling of the burial chamber are each made of a single monolith of cut stone, and on the boundary with the antechamber in the foreground there is an entrance carved out of a single piece of stone. A house-shaped sarcophagus is placed in the back. As the tomb has been open since antiquity, no [[grave goods]] have been found, although no modern [[archaeological excavation]] has been conducted. From the structure of the tumulus, it is believed to have been built around the 7th century, or the final stages of the Kofun period. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is 1.7 kilometers, or approximately five minutes by car from [[JR West]] [[San'in Main Line]] [[Izumoshi Station]].
<gallery> Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, 3D-1.jpg|3D rendition of the burial chamber Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, zenshitsu-1.jpg|Antechamber（looking towards burial chamber） Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, zenshitsu-2.jpg| Antechamber（looking toward entrance） Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, sendou.jpg|Corridor（looking towards burial chamber） Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun]][[en:Kamienyajizōyama Kofun]][[ja:上塩冶地蔵山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q60987962]] Wikidata name: Kamienya Jizōyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q60987962]] Wikidata name: Kamienya Jizōyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Kamienyajizōyama Kofun | native_name =上塩冶地蔵山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamienyajizōyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kamienyajizōyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.346111|N|132.759444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Izumo|qid=Q825166}} | region = [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|circular kofun|qid=Q11394747}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|20|45.95|N|132|45|34.15|E|}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kamienyajizōyama Kofun'''|上塩冶地蔵山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamienya-chō, neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Izumo|Izumo]] in the [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1924.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206143|title=上塩冶地蔵山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is ne of the Kofuns that make up the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Kamienyajizōyama Kofun is located on the north bank of the Kobe River, on a gentle slope on the west side of the Izumo Technical High School. in the northeastern part of Shimane Prefecture. Although the area around the tumulus has been largely leveled land clearing for [[pa11y field]]s, the tumulus is believed to be a square {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style ''kofun'', measuring 15 meters on each side, and with a height of five meters, although it may originally have been an oval-shaped tumulus. The horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] opens to the southeast, and consists of a burial chamber (back chamber), an antechamber, and a passageway. It was constructed with the same processed [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] stones as the [[Kamienyatsukiyama Kofun]] located 600 meters to the north. The burial chamber is about 9 meters long, 2.4 meters wide, and 2.25 meters high. The walls and ceiling of the burial chamber are each made of a single monolith of cut stone, and on the boundary with the antechamber in the foreground there is an entrance carved out of a single piece of stone. A house-shaped sarcophagus is placed in the back. As the tomb has been open since antiquity, no [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have been found, although no modern [[archaeological excavation]] has been conducted. From the structure of the tumulus, it is believed to have been built around the 7th century, or the final stages of the Kofun period. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is 1.7 kilometers, or approximately five minutes by car from [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]] [[:en:Izumoshi Station|Izumoshi Station]].
<gallery> Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, 3D-1.jpg|3D rendition of the burial chamber Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, zenshitsu-1.jpg|Antechamber（looking towards burial chamber） Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, zenshitsu-2.jpg| Antechamber（looking toward entrance） Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, sendou.jpg|Corridor（looking towards burial chamber） Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shimane)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{Commonscat|Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun}}

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1924.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206143|title=上塩冶地蔵山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is ne of the Kofuns that make up the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Kamienyajizōyama Kofun is located on the north bank of the Kobe River, on a gentle slope on the west side of the Izumo Technical High School. in the northeastern part of Shimane Prefecture. Although the area around the tumulus has been largely leveled land clearing for [[pa11y field]]s, the tumulus is believed to be a square {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style ''kofun'', measuring 15 meters on each side, and with a height of five meters, although it may originally have been an oval-shaped tumulus. The horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] opens to the southeast, and consists of a burial chamber (back chamber), an antechamber, and a passageway. It was constructed with the same processed [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] stones as the [[Kamienyatsukiyama Kofun]] located 600 meters to the north. The burial chamber is about 9 meters long, 2.4 meters wide, and 2.25 meters high. The walls and ceiling of the burial chamber are each made of a single monolith of cut stone, and on the boundary with the antechamber in the foreground there is an entrance carved out of a single piece of stone. A house-shaped sarcophagus is placed in the back. As the tomb has been open since antiquity, no [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have been found, although no modern [[archaeological excavation]] has been conducted. From the structure of the tumulus, it is believed to have been built around the 7th century, or the final stages of the Kofun period. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is 1.7 kilometers, or approximately five minutes by car from [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]] [[:en:Izumoshi Station|Izumoshi Station]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun]][[en:Kamienyajizōyama Kofun]][[ja:上塩冶地蔵山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q60987962]] Wikidata name: Kamienya Jizōyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q60987962]] Wikidata name: Kamienya Jizōyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:57, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Kamienyajizōyama Kofun | native_name =上塩冶地蔵山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamienyajizōyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kamienyajizōyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.346111|N|132.759444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Izumo|WD=Q825166}} | region = [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|20|45.95|N|132|45|34.15|E|}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kamienyajizōyama Kofun'''|上塩冶地蔵山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamienya-chō, neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Izumo|Izumo]] in the [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1924.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206143|title=上塩冶地蔵山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is ne of the Kofuns that make up the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Kamienyajizōyama Kofun is located on the north bank of the Kobe River, on a gentle slope on the west side of the Izumo Technical High School. in the northeastern part of Shimane Prefecture. Although the area around the tumulus has been largely leveled land clearing for [[pa11y field]]s, the tumulus is believed to be a square {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style ''kofun'', measuring 15 meters on each side, and with a height of five meters, although it may originally have been an oval-shaped tumulus. The horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] opens to the southeast, and consists of a burial chamber (back chamber), an antechamber, and a passageway. It was constructed with the same processed [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] stones as the [[Kamienyatsukiyama Kofun]] located 600 meters to the north. The burial chamber is about 9 meters long, 2.4 meters wide, and 2.25 meters high. The walls and ceiling of the burial chamber are each made of a single monolith of cut stone, and on the boundary with the antechamber in the foreground there is an entrance carved out of a single piece of stone. A house-shaped sarcophagus is placed in the back. As the tomb has been open since antiquity, no [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have been found, although no modern [[archaeological excavation]] has been conducted. From the structure of the tumulus, it is believed to have been built around the 7th century, or the final stages of the Kofun period. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is 1.7 kilometers, or approximately five minutes by car from [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]] [[:en:Izumoshi Station|Izumoshi Station]].
<gallery> Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, 3D-1.jpg|3D rendition of the burial chamber Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, zenshitsu-1.jpg|Antechamber（looking towards burial chamber） Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, zenshitsu-2.jpg| Antechamber（looking toward entrance） Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, sendou.jpg|Corridor（looking towards burial chamber） Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

The {{nihongo|'''Kamienyajizōyama Kofun'''|上塩冶地蔵山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamienya-chō, neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Izumo|Izumo]] in the [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1924.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206143|title=上塩冶地蔵山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is ne of the Kofuns that make up the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Kamienyajizōyama Kofun is located on the north bank of the Kobe River, on a gentle slope on the west side of the Izumo Technical High School. in the northeastern part of Shimane Prefecture. Although the area around the tumulus has been largely leveled land clearing for [[pa11y field]]s, the tumulus is believed to be a square {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style ''kofun'', measuring 15 meters on each side, and with a height of five meters, although it may originally have been an oval-shaped tumulus. The horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] opens to the southeast, and consists of a burial chamber (back chamber), an antechamber, and a passageway. It was constructed with the same processed [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] stones as the [[Kamienyatsukiyama Kofun]] located 600 meters to the north. The burial chamber is about 9 meters long, 2.4 meters wide, and 2.25 meters high. The walls and ceiling of the burial chamber are each made of a single monolith of cut stone, and on the boundary with the antechamber in the foreground there is an entrance carved out of a single piece of stone. A house-shaped sarcophagus is placed in the back. As the tomb has been open since antiquity, no [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have been found, although no modern [[archaeological excavation]] has been conducted. From the structure of the tumulus, it is believed to have been built around the 7th century, or the final stages of the Kofun period. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is 1.7 kilometers, or approximately five minutes by car from [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]] [[:en:Izumoshi Station|Izumoshi Station]].
<gallery> Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, 3D-1.jpg|3D rendition of the burial chamber Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, zenshitsu-1.jpg|Antechamber（looking towards burial chamber） Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, zenshitsu-2.jpg| Antechamber（looking toward entrance） Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, sendou.jpg|Corridor（looking towards burial chamber） Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

As the tomb has been open since antiquity, no [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have been found, although no modern [[archaeological excavation]] has been conducted. From the structure of the tumulus, it is believed to have been built around the 7th century, or the final stages of the Kofun period. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

The {{nihongo|'''Kamienyajizōyama Kofun'''|上塩冶地蔵山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamienya-chō, neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Izumo|Izumo]] in the [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1924.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206143|title=上塩冶地蔵山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is ne of the Kofuns that make up the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Kamienyajizōyama Kofun is located on the north bank of the Kobe River, on a gentle slope on the west side of the Izumo Technical High School. in the northeastern part of Shimane Prefecture. Although the area around the tumulus has been largely leveled land clearing for [[pa11y field]]s, the tumulus is believed to be a square {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style ''kofun'', measuring 15 meters on each side, and with a height of five meters, although it may originally have been an oval-shaped tumulus. The horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] opens to the southeast, and consists of a burial chamber (back chamber), an antechamber, and a passageway. It was constructed with the same processed [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] stones as the [[Kamienyatsukiyama Kofun]] located 600 meters to the north. The burial chamber is about 9 meters long, 2.4 meters wide, and 2.25 meters high. The walls and ceiling of the burial chamber are each made of a single monolith of cut stone, and on the boundary with the antechamber in the foreground there is an entrance carved out of a single piece of stone. A house-shaped sarcophagus is placed in the back. As the tomb has been open since antiquity, no [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have been found, although no modern [[archaeological excavation]] has been conducted. From the structure of the tumulus, it is believed to have been built around the 7th century, or the final stages of the Kofun period. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is 1.7 kilometers, or approximately five minutes by car from [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]] [[:en:Izumoshi Station|Izumoshi Station]].

The {{nihongo|'''Kamienyajizōyama Kofun'''|上塩冶地蔵山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamienya-chō, neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Izumo|Izumo]] in the [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1924.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206143|title=上塩冶地蔵山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is ne of the Kofuns that make up the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Kamienyajizōyama Kofun is located on the north bank of the Kobe River, on a gentle slope on the west side of the Izumo Technical High School. in the northeastern part of Shimane Prefecture. Although the area around the tumulus has been largely leveled land clearing for [[pa11y field]]s, the tumulus is believed to be a square {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style ''kofun'', measuring 15 meters on each side, and with a height of five meters, although it may originally have been an oval-shaped tumulus. The horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] opens to the southeast, and consists of a burial chamber (back chamber), an antechamber, and a passageway. It was constructed with the same processed [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] stones as the [[Kamienyatsukiyama Kofun]] located 600 meters to the north. The burial chamber is about 9 meters long, 2.4 meters wide, and 2.25 meters high. The walls and ceiling of the burial chamber are each made of a single monolith of cut stone, and on the boundary with the antechamber in the foreground there is an entrance carved out of a single piece of stone. A house-shaped sarcophagus is placed in the back. As the tomb has been open since antiquity, no [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have been found, although no modern [[archaeological excavation]] has been conducted. From the structure of the tumulus, it is believed to have been built around the 7th century, or the final stages of the Kofun period. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is 1.7 kilometers, or approximately five minutes by car from [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]] [[:en:Izumoshi Station|Izumoshi Station]].

The tumulus is 1.7 kilometers, or approximately five minutes by car from [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]] [[:en:Izumoshi Station|Izumoshi Station]].

The {{nihongo|'''Kamienyajizōyama Kofun'''|上塩冶地蔵山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamienya-chō, neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Izumo|Izumo]] in the [[:en:San'in region|San'in region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1924.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206143|title=上塩冶地蔵山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is ne of the Kofuns that make up the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Kamienyajizōyama Kofun is located on the north bank of the Kobe River, on a gentle slope on the west side of the Izumo Technical High School. in the northeastern part of Shimane Prefecture. Although the area around the tumulus has been largely leveled land clearing for [[pa11y field]]s, the tumulus is believed to be a square {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style ''kofun'', measuring 15 meters on each side, and with a height of five meters, although it may originally have been an oval-shaped tumulus. The horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] opens to the southeast, and consists of a burial chamber (back chamber), an antechamber, and a passageway. It was constructed with the same processed [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] stones as the [[Kamienyatsukiyama Kofun]] located 600 meters to the north. The burial chamber is about 9 meters long, 2.4 meters wide, and 2.25 meters high. The walls and ceiling of the burial chamber are each made of a single monolith of cut stone, and on the boundary with the antechamber in the foreground there is an entrance carved out of a single piece of stone. A house-shaped sarcophagus is placed in the back. As the tomb has been open since antiquity, no [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] have been found, although no modern [[archaeological excavation]] has been conducted. From the structure of the tumulus, it is believed to have been built around the 7th century, or the final stages of the Kofun period. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is 1.7 kilometers, or approximately five minutes by car from [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:San'in Main Line|San'in Main Line]] [[:en:Izumoshi Station|Izumoshi Station]].
<gallery> Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, 3D-1.jpg|3D rendition of the burial chamber Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, zenshitsu-1.jpg|Antechamber（looking towards burial chamber） Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, zenshitsu-2.jpg| Antechamber（looking toward entrance） Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, sendou.jpg|Corridor（looking towards burial chamber） Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

The {{nihongo|'''Kamienyajizōyama Kofun'''|上塩冶地蔵山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamienya-chō, neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Izumo|en|Izumo|WD=Q301580|lt=Izumo}} in the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1924.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206143|title=上塩冶地蔵山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is ne of the Kofuns that make up the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Kamienyajizōyama Kofun is located on the north bank of the Kobe River, on a gentle slope on the west side of the Izumo Technical High School. in the northeastern part of Shimane Prefecture. Although the area around the tumulus has been largely leveled land clearing for [[pa11y field]]s, the tumulus is believed to be a square {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style ''kofun'', measuring 15 meters on each side, and with a height of five meters, although it may originally have been an oval-shaped tumulus. The horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] opens to the southeast, and consists of a burial chamber (back chamber), an antechamber, and a passageway. It was constructed with the same processed {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff}} stones as the [[Kamienyatsukiyama Kofun]] located 600 meters to the north. The burial chamber is about 9 meters long, 2.4 meters wide, and 2.25 meters high. The walls and ceiling of the burial chamber are each made of a single monolith of cut stone, and on the boundary with the antechamber in the foreground there is an entrance carved out of a single piece of stone. A house-shaped sarcophagus is placed in the back. As the tomb has been open since antiquity, no {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} have been found, although no modern [[archaeological excavation]] has been conducted. From the structure of the tumulus, it is believed to have been built around the 7th century, or the final stages of the Kofun period. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is 1.7 kilometers, or approximately five minutes by car from {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line}} {{ill|Izumoshi Station|en|Izumoshi Station|WD=Q860288|lt=Izumoshi Station}}.
<gallery> Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, 3D-1.jpg|3D rendition of the burial chamber Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, zenshitsu-1.jpg|Antechamber（looking towards burial chamber） Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, zenshitsu-2.jpg| Antechamber（looking toward entrance） Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, sendou.jpg|Corridor（looking towards burial chamber） Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

Although the area around the tumulus has been largely leveled land clearing for [[pa11y field]]s, the tumulus is believed to be a square {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style ''kofun'', measuring 15 meters on each side, and with a height of five meters, although it may originally have been an oval-shaped tumulus. The horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] opens to the southeast, and consists of a burial chamber (back chamber), an antechamber, and a passageway. It was constructed with the same processed {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff}} stones as the [[Kamienyatsukiyama Kofun]] located 600 meters to the north. The burial chamber is about 9 meters long, 2.4 meters wide, and 2.25 meters high. The walls and ceiling of the burial chamber are each made of a single monolith of cut stone, and on the boundary with the antechamber in the foreground there is an entrance carved out of a single piece of stone. A house-shaped sarcophagus is placed in the back. As the tomb has been open since antiquity, no {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} have been found, although no modern [[archaeological excavation]] has been conducted. From the structure of the tumulus, it is believed to have been built around the 7th century, or the final stages of the Kofun period. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is 1.7 kilometers, or approximately five minutes by car from {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line}} {{ill|Izumoshi Station|en|Izumoshi Station|WD=Q860288|lt=Izumoshi Station}}.

{{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Square Kofun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|zh|方墳|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name = Kamienyajizōyama Kofun | native_name =上塩冶地蔵山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Kamienyajizōyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shimane Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Kamienyajizōyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.346111|N|132.759444|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Izumo|WD=Q825166|1=Izumo, Shimane}} | region = {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.7th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|20|45.95|N|132|45|34.15|E|}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Kamienyajizōyama Kofun'''|上塩冶地蔵山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamienya-chō, neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Izumo|en|Izumo|WD=Q301580|lt=Izumo|1=Izumo}} in the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1924.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206143|title=上塩冶地蔵山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is ne of the Kofuns that make up the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Kamienyajizōyama Kofun is located on the north bank of the Kobe River, on a gentle slope on the west side of the Izumo Technical High School. in the northeastern part of Shimane Prefecture. Although the area around the tumulus has been largely leveled land clearing for [[pa11y field]]s, the tumulus is believed to be a square {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style ''kofun'', measuring 15 meters on each side, and with a height of five meters, although it may originally have been an oval-shaped tumulus. The horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] opens to the southeast, and consists of a burial chamber (back chamber), an antechamber, and a passageway. It was constructed with the same processed {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff|1=Tuff}} stones as the [[Kamienyatsukiyama Kofun]] located 600 meters to the north. The burial chamber is about 9 meters long, 2.4 meters wide, and 2.25 meters high. The walls and ceiling of the burial chamber are each made of a single monolith of cut stone, and on the boundary with the antechamber in the foreground there is an entrance carved out of a single piece of stone. A house-shaped sarcophagus is placed in the back. As the tomb has been open since antiquity, no {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} have been found, although no modern [[archaeological excavation]] has been conducted. From the structure of the tumulus, it is believed to have been built around the 7th century, or the final stages of the Kofun period. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is 1.7 kilometers, or approximately five minutes by car from {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line|1=San'in Main Line}} {{ill|Izumoshi Station|en|Izumoshi Station|WD=Q860288|lt=Izumoshi Station|1=Izumoshi Station}}.
<gallery> Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, 3D-1.jpg|3D rendition of the burial chamber Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, zenshitsu-1.jpg|Antechamber（looking towards burial chamber） Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, zenshitsu-2.jpg| Antechamber（looking toward entrance） Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, sendou.jpg|Corridor（looking towards burial chamber） Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>

The {{nihongo|'''Kamienyajizōyama Kofun'''|上塩冶地蔵山古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kamienya-chō, neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Izumo|en|Izumo|WD=Q301580|lt=Izumo|1=Izumo}} in the {{ill|San'in region|en|San'in region|WD=Q398310|lt=San'in region|1=San'in region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1924.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206143|title=上塩冶地蔵山古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> It is ne of the Kofuns that make up the Tsukiyama Kofun Cluster.
==Overview== The Kamienyajizōyama Kofun is located on the north bank of the Kobe River, on a gentle slope on the west side of the Izumo Technical High School. in the northeastern part of Shimane Prefecture. Although the area around the tumulus has been largely leveled land clearing for [[pa11y field]]s, the tumulus is believed to be a square {{nihongo|''{{ill|Square Kofun|lt=hōfun|ja|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Square Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}''|方墳|1=Square Kofun|1=Square Kofun}}-style ''kofun'', measuring 15 meters on each side, and with a height of five meters, although it may originally have been an oval-shaped tumulus. The horizontal stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] opens to the southeast, and consists of a burial chamber (back chamber), an antechamber, and a passageway. It was constructed with the same processed {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff|1=Tuff}} stones as the [[Kamienyatsukiyama Kofun]] located 600 meters to the north. The burial chamber is about 9 meters long, 2.4 meters wide, and 2.25 meters high. The walls and ceiling of the burial chamber are each made of a single monolith of cut stone, and on the boundary with the antechamber in the foreground there is an entrance carved out of a single piece of stone. A house-shaped sarcophagus is placed in the back. As the tomb has been open since antiquity, no {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} have been found, although no modern [[archaeological excavation]] has been conducted. From the structure of the tumulus, it is believed to have been built around the 7th century, or the final stages of the Kofun period. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The tumulus is 1.7 kilometers, or approximately five minutes by car from {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company|1=West Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|San'in Main Line|en|San'in Main Line|WD=Q1135170|lt=San'in Main Line|1=San'in Main Line}} {{ill|Izumoshi Station|en|Izumoshi Station|WD=Q860288|lt=Izumoshi Station|1=Izumoshi Station}}.
<gallery> Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, 3D-1.jpg|3D rendition of the burial chamber Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, zenshitsu-1.jpg|Antechamber（looking towards burial chamber） Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, zenshitsu-2.jpg| Antechamber（looking toward entrance） Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, sendou.jpg|Corridor（looking towards burial chamber） Kamienya Jizoyama Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance </gallery>
